hero.title:
Music Theory
Courses
hero.sub: 8 progressive courses that take you from first note to complete musical fluency — for every age and level.

courses.title: Music Fundamentals
courses.desc: Notes, clefs, staff, basic notation & first reading

courses.title: Rhythm & Meter
courses.desc: Time signatures, beats, subdivision & rhythmic reading

courses.title: Scales & Modes
courses.desc: Major, minor & all 7 modal scales explored in depth

courses.title: Intervals & Ear Training
courses.desc: Recognize, sing & notate any musical interval with precision

courses.title: Voice Leading
courses.desc: Connect chords with smooth, resonant inner voices

courses.title: Composition & Form
courses.desc: Song structure, musical analysis & original composition

coursesSection.label: What You'll Learn
coursesSection.sub: From the very first note to full composition — a clear, progressive path with specialist instructors.

features.title: Expert Faculty
features.desc: Every instructor holds a conservatory degree and performs professionally. Real musicianship, real mentorship.

features.title: All Instruments
features.desc: Twelve instruments across classical, contemporary, and electronic genres. One curriculum, infinite paths.

features.title: Flexible Scheduling
features.desc: Mornings, evenings, weekends. In-studio or online. We fit around your life, not the other way around.

features.title: Performance Opportunities
features.desc: Recitals, workshops, and masterclasses year-round. Students become confident performers, not just players.

list.name: Maya Chen
list.title: Piano & Composition
list.bio: A Juilliard graduate and concert pianist with 14 years of teaching experience. Maya specializes in bridging classical technique with modern musical sensibility.

list.name: Rafael Torres
list.title: Guitar & Music Production
list.bio: Berklee alumnus, session guitarist, and producer with credits on three Billboard-charting records. Rafael brings real-world industry knowledge to every lesson.

list.name: Aisha Okafor
list.title: Voice & Performance
list.bio: Royal College of Music graduate and active West End performer. Aisha's vocal method blends classical bel canto with contemporary technique.

list.quote: I joined with zero musical background at 34. Within six months I was playing pieces I had always dreamed of. Maya's patience and method are nothing short of extraordinary.
list.name: Sarah Kimura

list.quote: My daughter has been with the academy for three years. She performed her first recital at age 7 and just won her regional youth violin competition.
list.name: David Moreau

list.quote: Rafael didn't just teach me guitar — he showed me how music production works end-to-end. I released my first EP at 19 thanks to what I learned here.
list.name: Jordan Achebe

faq.label: FAQ
faq.intro: Can't find what you're looking for? Reach out directly and we'll be happy to help.

list.q: What age can my child start music lessons?
list.a: We accept children from age 3 for our early childhood programs. Individual instrument lessons typically begin at age 5–7 depending on the instrument. Our faculty recommends the best starting point during a free assessment.
Piano, Guitar (acoustic, electric, classical), Violin, Viola, Drums, Voice, Trumpet, Flute, Saxophone, Music Theory, and Music Production. New instruments are added based on demand.

list.q: What happens during the free trial lesson?
list.a: Your first lesson is completely free and commitment-free. The instructor assesses your current level, discusses your goals, introduces foundational concepts, and recommends a learning path.

list.q: What is your cancellation and rescheduling policy?
list.a: Lessons cancelled at least 24 hours in advance can be rescheduled at no charge. Late cancellations within 24 hours are charged at 50% of the lesson rate. Members can pause for up to one month per year without penalty.

list.q: How much should students practice at home?
list.a: Beginners: 15–20 minutes daily. Intermediate: 30–45 minutes. Advanced: 60–90 minutes. Consistency matters far more than duration; short daily sessions outperform weekly marathons every time.
